title: The Kavanagh.
content:
A stone jug and a pewter mug,
    And a table set for three!
    A jug and a mug at every place,
    And a biscuit or two with Brie!
    Three

title: Buie Annajohn.
content:
Buie Annajohn was the king's black mare,
    Buie, Buie, Buie Annajohn!
    Satin was her coat and silk was her hair,
    Buie Annajohn,
    T

title: In The Workshop.
content:
Once in the Workshop, ages ago,
    The clay was wet and the fire was low.

    And He who was bent on fashioning man
    Moulded a shape from

title: Concerning Kavin.
content:
When Kavin comes back from the barber,
    Although he no longer is young,
    One cheek is as soft as his heart,
    And the other as smooth a

title: Contemporaries.
content:
"A barbered woman's man,"--yes, so
    He seemed to me a twelvemonth since;
    And so he may be--let it go--
    Admit his flaws--we need not

title: Laurana's Song. For "A Lady Of Venice."
content:
Who'll have the crumpled pieces of a heart?
    Let him take mine!
    Who'll give his whole of passion for a part,
    And call't divine?

title: A More Ancient Mariner.
content:
The swarthy bee is a buccaneer,
    A burly velveted rover,
    Who loves the booming wind in his ear
    As he sails the seas of clover.

title: The Face In The Stream
content:
The sunburnt face in the willow shade
    To the face in the water-mirror said,

    "O deep mysterious face in the stream,
    Art thou myself

title: The Moondial
content:
Iron and granite and rust,
    In a crumbling garden old,
    Where the roses are paler than dust
    And the lilies are green with gold,

title: Nancibel.
content:
The ghost of a wind came over the hill,
    While day for a moment forgot to die,
    And stirred the sheaves
    Of the millet leaves,
    As

title: June Night In Washington.
content:
The scent of honeysuckle,
    Drugging the twilight
    With its sweet opiate of lovers' dreams!
    The last red glow of the setting sun

title: Mary Of Marka.
content:
Eric of Marka holds the knife:
    "A nameless death for a nameless life."--

    "Mary of Marka, bid him stay,
    And the morrow shall be our

title: September Woodlands.
content:
This is not sadness in the wood;
    The yellowbird
    Flits joying through the solitude,
    By no thought stirred
    Save of his little du

title: The Two Bobbies.
content:
Bobbie Burns and Bobbie Browning,
    They're the boys I'd like to see.
    Though I'm not the boy for Bobbie,
    Bobbie is the boy for me!

title: A Rover's Song.
content:
Snowdrift of the mountains,
    Spindrift of the sea,
    We who down the border
    Rove from gloom to glee,--

    Snowdrift of the mountain

title: A Good-By.
content:
For love of the roving foot
    And joy of the roving eye,
    God send you store of morrows fair
    And a good rest by and by!

title: In The House Of Idiedaily.
content:
Oh, but life went gayly, gayly,
    In the house of Idiedaily!

    There were always throats to sing
    Down the river-banks with spring,

title: Behind The Arras
content:
I like the old house tolerably well,
    Where I must dwell
    Like a familiar gnome;
    And yet I never shall feel quite at home:
    I lov

title: Shakespeare Himself: For The Unveiling Of Mr. Partridge'S Statue Of The Poet.
content:
The body is no prison where we lie
    Shut out from our true heritage of sun;
    It is the wings wherewith the soul may fly.
    Save through

title: To G. H. B.
content:
"I shut myself in with my soul,
    And the shapes come eddying forth."

title: Premonition.
content:
He said, "Good-night, my heart is light,
    To-morrow morn at day
    We two together in the dew
    Shall forth and fare away.

    "We shal

title: A Son Of The Sea
The Mother Of Poets. To H. F. H.
The Bather.
content:
I saw him go down to the water to bathe;
    He stood naked upon the bank.

    His breast was like a white cloud in the heaven,
    that catch

title: At The End Of The Day.
content:
There is no escape by the river,
    There is no flight left by the fen;
    We are compassed about by the shiver
    Of the night of their mar

title: Arnold, Master Of The Scud
content:
There's a schooner out from Kingsport,
    Through the morning's dazzle-gleam,
    Snoring down the Bay of Fundy
    With a norther on her beam

title: In The Wayland Willows.
content:
Once I met a soncy maid,
    Soncy maid, soncy maid,
    Once I met a soncy maid
    In the Wayland willows.

    All her hair was goldy brown

title: The Mocking-Bird.
content:
Hear! hear! hear!
    Listen! the word
    Of the mocking-bird!
    Hear! hear! hear!
    I will make all clear;
    I will let you know

title: Daisies.
content:
Over the shoulders and slopes of the dune
    I saw the white daisies go down to the sea,
    A host in the sunshine, an army in June,
    The

title: Noons Of Poppy
content:
Noons of poppy, noons of poppy,
    Scarlet leagues along the sea;
    Flaxen hair afloat in sunlight,
    Love, come down the world to me!

title: Hem And Haw.
content:
Hem and Haw were the sons of sin,
    Created to shally and shirk;
    Hem lay 'round and Haw looked on
    While God did all the work.

    H

title: Legends Of Lost Haven
content:
There are legends of Lost Haven,
    Come, I know not whence, to me,
    When the wind is in the clover,
    When the sun is on the sea.

title: The Faun. A Fragment.
content:
I will go out to grass with that old King,
    For I am weary of clothes and cooks.
    I long to lie along the banks of brooks,
    And watch

title: A Captain Of The Press-Gang.
content:
Shipmate, leave the ghostly shadows,
    Where thy boon companions throng!
    We will put to sea together
    Through the twilight with a song

title: The Mote.
content:
Two shapes of august bearing, seraph tall,
    Of indolent imperturbable regard,
    Stood in the Tavern door to drink. As the first
    Lifted

title: The Last Watch
content:
Comrades, comrades, have me buried
    Like a warrior of the sea,
    With a flag across my breast
    And my sword upon my knee.

    Steerin

title: Down The Songo.
content:
I.

    Floating!
    Floating--and all the stillness waits
    And listens at the ivory gates,
    Full of a dim uncertain presage
    Of some s

title: The Kelpie Riders
content:
I

    Buried alive in calm Rochelle,
    Six in a row by a crystal well,

    All Summer long on Bareau Fen
    Slumber and sleep the Kelpie men;

title: Evening On The Potomac.
content:
The fervid breath of our flushed Southern May
    Is sweet upon the city's throat and lips,
    As a lover's whose tired arm slips
    Listless

title: A Faun's Song.
content:
Cool! cool! cool!
    Cool and sweet
    The feel of the moss at my feet!
    And sweet and cool
    The touch of the wind, of the wind!

title: A Hill Song.
content:
Hills where once my love and I
    Let the hours go laughing by!
    All your woods and dales are sad,--
    You have lost your Oread.
    Fal

title: The Ships Of St. John
content:
Smile, you inland hills and rivers!
    Flush, you mountains in the dawn!
    But my roving heart is seaward
    With the ships of gray St. Joh

title: Exit Anima
content:
"Hospes comesque corporis,
    Quae nunc abitis in loca?"

    Cease, Wind, to blow
    And drive the peopled snow,
    And move the haunted a

title: Secrets.
content:
Three secrets that never were said:
    The stir of the sap in the spring,
    The desire of a man to a maid,
    The urge of a poet to sing.

title: The Buccaneers.
content:
Oh, not for us the easy mirth
    Of men that never roam!
    The crackling of the narrow hearth,
    The cabined joys of home!
    Keep your

title: The Gravedigger
content:
Oh, the shambling sea is a sexton old,
    And well his work is done.
    With an equal grave for lord and knave,
    He buries them every one.

title: At The Granite Gate
content:
There paused to shut the door
    A fellow called the Wind.
    With mystery before,
    And reticence behind,

    A portal waits me too

title: Across The Table. To A. L. L.
content:
Here's to you, Arthur! You and I
    Have seen a lot of stormy weather,
    Since first we clinked cups on the sly
    At school together.

title: Beyond The Gamut
content:
Softly, softly, Niccolo Amati!
    What can put such fancies in your head?
    There, go dream of your blue-skied Cremona,
    While I ponder s

title: A Friend's Wish. To C. W. S.
content:
Give me your last Aloha,
    When I go out of sight,
    Over the dark rim of the sea
    Into the Polar night!

    And all the Northland giv

title: Hack And Hew
content:
Hack and Hew were the sons of God
    In the earlier earth than now;
    One at his right hand, one at his left,
    To obey as he taught them

title: Comrades.
content:
Comrades, pour the wine to-night
    For the parting is with dawn!
    Oh, the clink of cups together,
    With the daylight coming on!
    Gr

title: Barney Mcgee.
content:
Barney McGee, there's no end of good luck in you,
    Will-o'-the-wisp, with a flicker of Puck in you,
    Wild as a bull-pup and all of his plu

title: A Song By The Shore.
content:
"Lose and love" is love's first art;
    So it was with thee and me,
    For I first beheld thy heart
    On the night I last saw thee.
    Pi

title: The Dustman
content:
"Dustman, dustman!"
    Through the deserted square he cries,
    And babies put their rosy fists
    Into their eyes.

    There's nothing ou

title: Verlaine.
content:
Avid of life and love, insatiate vagabond,
    With quest too furious for the graal he would have won,
    He flung himself at the eternal sky,

title: Mr. Moon: A Song Of The Little People.
content:
O Moon, Mr. Moon,
    When you comin' down?
    Down on the hilltop,
    Down in the glen,
    Out in the clearin',
    To play with little m

title: The Cruise Of The Galleon
content:
This laboring vast, Tellurian Galleon,
    Riding at anchor off the orient sun,
    Had broken its cable, and stood out to space.

    FRANCIS

title: In The Wings
content:
The play is Life; and this round earth,
    The narrow stage whereon
    We act before an audience
    Of actors dead and gone.

    There is

title: In A Garden.
content:
Thought is a garden wide and old
    For airy creatures to explore,
    Where grow the great fantastic flowers
    With truth for honey at the

title: The Faithless Lover
content:
I

    O Life, dear Life, in this fair house
    Long since did I, it seems to me,
    In some mysterious doleful way
    Fall out of love with th

title: The Yule Guest
content:
And Yanna by the yule log
    Sat in the empty hall,
    And watched the goblin firelight
    Caper upon the wall:

    The goblins of the hea

title: Lal Of Kilrudden.
content:
Kilrudden ford, Kilrudden dale,
    Kilrudden fronting every gale
    On the lorn coast of Inishfree,
    And Lal's last bed the plunging sea.

title: Kavin Again.
content:
It is not anything he says,
    It's just his presence and his smile,
    The blarney of his silences
    That cocker and beguile.

title: Fancy's Fool
content:
"Cornel, cornel, green and white,
    Spreading on the forest floor,
    Whither went my lost delight
    Through the silent door?"

    "Mort

title: Nocturne: In Anjou.
content:
I dreamed of Sappho on a summer night.
    Her nightingales were singing in the trees
    Beside the castled river; and the wind
    Fell like

title: The Lodger
content:
I cannot quite recall
    When first he came,
    So reticent and tall,
    With his eyes of flame.

    The neighbors used to say
    (They

title: The Night Express
content:
Out through the hills of midnight,
    Hurtling and thundering on,
    The night express from the outer world
    Speeds for the open of dawn.

title: Distillation.
content:
They that eat the uncrushed grape
    Walk with steady heels:
    Lo, now, how they stare and gape
    Where the poet reels!
    He has drunk

title: An Easter Market.
content:
Today, through your Easter market
    In the lazy Southern sun,
    I strolled with hands in pockets
    Past the flower-stalls one by one.

title: A Toast.
content:
Here's a health to thee, Roberts,
    And here's a health to me;
    And here's to all the pretty girls
    From Denver to the sea!

    Here'

title: Karlene.
content:
Word of a little one born in the West,--
    How like a sea-bird it comes from the sea,
    Out of the league-weary waters' unrest
    Blown wi

title: The King's Son.
content:
"Daughter, daughter, marry no man,
    Though a king's son come to woo,
    If he be not more than blessing or ban
    To the secret soul of yo

title: Discovery.
content:
When the bugler morn shall wind his horn,
    And we wake to the wild to be,
    Shall we open our eyes on the selfsame skies
    And stare at

title: The Marring Of Malyn
content:
I

                The Merrymakers

    Among the wintry mountains beside the Northern sea
    There is a merrymaking, as old as old can be.

title: The Night-Washers.
content:
Whe-ooh, ooh, ooh, ooh, ooh!
    We are the brothers of ghouls, and who
    In the name of the Crooked Saints are you?

    We are the washers

title: The Joys Of The Road.
content:
Now the joys of the road are chiefly these:
    A crimson touch on the hard-wood trees;

    A vagrant's morning wide and blue,
    In early fa

title: Nocturne: In Provence.
content:
The blue night, like an angel, came into the room,--
    Came through the open window from the silent sky
    Down trellised stairs of moonlight

title: The Sleepers
content:
The tall carnations down the garden walks
    Bowed on their stalks.

    Said Jock-a-dreams to John-a-nods,
    "What are the odds
    That w

title: Jongleurs.
content:
What is the stir in the street?
    Hurry of feet!
    And after,
    A sound as of pipes and of tabers!

    Men of the conflicts and labors,

title: At Michaelmas.
content:
About the time of Michael's feast
    And all his angels,
    There comes a word to man and beast
    By dark evangels.

    Then hearing what

title: A Stein Song.
content:
Give a rouse, then, in the Maytime
    For a life that knows no fear!
    Turn night-time into daytime
    With the sunlight of good cheer!

title: A Song Before Sailing
content:
"Cras ingens iterabimus aequor."

    Wind of the dead men's feet,
    Blow down the empty street
    Of this old city by the sea
    With new

title: In A Silence
content:
Heart to heart!
    And the stillness of night and the moonlight, like hushed breathing
    Silently, stealthily moving across thy hair!

    O

title: At The Road-House: In Memory Of Robert Louis Stevenson.
content:
You hearken, fellows? Turned aside
    Into the road-house of the past!
    The prince of vagabonds is gone
    To house among his peers at las

title: Resignation.
content:
When I am only fit to go to bed,
    Or hobble out to sit within the sun,
    Ring down the curtain, say the play is done,
    And the last pet

title: Isabel.
content:
In her body's perfect sweet
    Suppleness and languor meet,--
    Arms that move like lapsing billows,
    Breasts that Love would make his pi

title: Speech And Silence.
content:
The words that pass from lip to lip
    For souls still out of reach!
    A friend for that companionship
    That's deeper than all speech!

title: The War-Song Of Gamelbar.
content:
Bowmen, shout for Gamelbar!
    Winds, unthrottle the wolves of war!
    Heave a breath
    And dare a death
    For the doom of Gamelbar!

title: Earth's Lyric.
content:
April. You hearken, my fellow,
    Old slumberer down in my heart?
    There's a whooping of ice in the rivers;
    The sap feels a start.

title: The Wander-Lovers.
content:
Down the world with Marna!
    That's the life for me!
    Wandering with the wandering wind,
    Vagabond and unconfined!
    Roving with the

title: The Marching Morrows.
content:
Now gird thee well for courage,
    My knight of twenty year,
    Against the marching morrows
    That fill the world with fear!

    The flo

title: In A Copy Of Browning.
content:
Browning, old fellow,
    Your leaves grow yellow,
    Beginning to mellow
    As seasons pass.
    Your cover is wrinkled,
    And stained a

title: The Wood-God.
content:
Brother, lost brother!
    Thou of mine ancient kin!
    Thou of the swift will that no ponderings smother!
    The dumb life in me fumbles out

title: Hunting-Song: From "King Arthur."
content:
Oh, who would stay indoor, indoor,
    When the horn is on the hill? (Bugle: Tarantara!
    With the crisp air stinging, and the huntsmen singin

title: A Waif.
content:
Do you know what it is to be vagrant born?
    A waif is only a waif. And so,
    For another idle hour I sit,
    In large content while the f

title: A Vagabond Song.
content:
There is something in the autumn that is native to my blood--
    Touch of manner, hint of mood;
    And my heart is like a rhyme,
    With the

title: A Song For Marna.
content:
Dame of the night of hair
    Like blue smoke blown!
    World yet undreamed-of there
    Lurks to be known.

    Dame of the dizzy eyes,

title: The Shadow Boatswain
content:
Don't you know the sailing orders?
    It is time to put to sea,
    And the stranger in the harbor
    Sends a boat ashore for me.

    With

title: Launa Dee.
content:
Weary, oh, so weary
    With it all!
    Sunny days or dreary--
    How they pall!
    Why should we be heroes,
    Launa Dee,
    Striving

title: The Nancy's Pride
content:
On the long slow heave of a lazy sea,
    To the flap of an idle sail,
    The Nancy's Pride went out on the tide;
    And the skipper stood by

title: Quince To Lilac: To G. H.
content:
Dear Lilac, how enchanting
    To hear of you this way!
    The Man who comes a-mouching
    To visit me each day

    Says you too have a lov

title: Spring Song.
content:
Make me over, mother April,
    When the sap begins to stir!
    When thy flowery hand delivers
    All the mountain-prisoned rivers,
    And

title: Three Of A Kind.
content:
Three of us without a care
    In the red September
    Tramping down the roads of Maine,
    Making merry with the rain,
    With the fellow

title: The Juggler
content:
Look how he throws them up and up,
    The beautiful golden balls!
    They hang aloft in the purple air,
    And there never is one that falls

title: The First Julep.
content:
I love the lazy Southern spring,
    The way she melts around a chap
    And lets the great magnolias fling
    Their languid petals in his lap

title: Vagabondia.
content:
Off with the fetters
    That chafe and restrain!
    Off with the chain!
    Here Art and Letters,
    Music and wine,
    And Myrtle and Wa

title: The Master Of The Isles
content:
There is rumor in Dark Harbor,
    And the folk are all astir;
    For a stranger in the offing
    Draws them down to gaze at her,

    In th

title: The Outlaw.
content:
Oh, let my lord laugh in his halls
    When he the tale shall tell!
    But woe to Jarlwell and its walls
    When I shall laugh as well!

title: Accident In Art.
content:
That painter has not with a careless smutch
    Accomplished his despair?--one touch revealing
    All he had put of life, thought, vigor, feeli

title: Outbound
content:
A lonely sail in the vast sea-room,
    I have put out for the port of gloom.

    The voyage is far on the trackless tide,
    The watch is lo

title: The Red Wolf
content:
With the fall of the leaf comes the wolf, wolf, wolf,
    The old red wolf at my door.
    And my hateful yellow dwarf, with his hideous crooked

title: The Hearse-Horse.
content:
Said the hearse-horse to the coffin,
    "What the devil have you there?
    I may trot from court to square,
    Yet it neither swears nor gro

title: At Sea.
content:
As a brave man faces the foe,
    Alone against hundreds, and sees Death grin in his teeth,
    But, shutting his lips, fights on to the end

title: Wood-Folk Lore. To T. B. M.
content:
For every one
    Beneath the sun,
    Where Autumn walks with quiet eyes,
    There is a word,
    Just overheard
    When hill to purple hi

title: The Unsainting Of Kavin.
content:
Saint Kavin was a gentleman,
    He came from Tipperary;
    And woman was the only thing
    That ever made him scary.

    For Kavin was a t

title: The King Of Ys
content:
Wild across the Breton country,
    Fabled centuries ago,
    Riding from the black sea border,
    Came the squadrons of the snow.

    Pipin

title: The Mendicants.
content:
We are as mendicants who wait
    Along the roadside in the sun.
    Tatters of yesterday and shreds
    Of morrow clothe us every one.

    A

title: The Crimson House
content:
Love built a crimson house,
    I know it well,
    That he might have a home
    Wherein to dwell.

    Poor Love that roved so far
    And

title: When I Was Twenty.
content:
It was June, and I was twenty.
    All my wisdom, poor but plenty,
    Never learned Festina lente.
    Youth is gone, but whither went he?

title: The Sea Gypsy.
content:
I am fevered with the sunset,
    I am fretful with the bay,
    For the wander-thirst is on me
    And my soul is in Cathay.

    There's a s
